# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Virtual-hosting (vh) fixtures: standard newlib + toolchain libgloss_vh
# provides semihosting-based _write/_exit/_sbrk. No custom runtime/libgloss.c
# in the link, so the binary carries zero vsim-specific MMIO paths and runs
# on any RISC-V target where -semihosting-config enable=on is honoured.
RT_C_VH_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello_vh
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# vh fixtures: crt0+trap+handler only (NO libgloss.c). -nodefaultlibs
# suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc; we re-add them
# in --start-group with -lgloss_vh inserted so newlib's syscall stubs
# resolve to the semihosting (virtual-hosting) implementations.
RT_C_VH_CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
RT_C_VH_RUNTIME := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler_vh.c
RT_C_VH_LIBS    := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# vh fixtures: identical layout to RT_C_FIXTURES but link against
# -lgloss_vh instead of the custom runtime/libgloss.c.
$(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_VH_RUNTIME) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_VH_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RT_C_VH_RUNTIME) $< $(RT_C_VH_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain and rt_phase_vsim_drain)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed
# separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES   := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Virtual-hosting (vh) fixtures: standard newlib + toolchain libgloss_vh
# provides semihosting-based _write/_exit/_sbrk. No custom runtime/libgloss.c
# in the link, so the binary carries zero vsim-specific MMIO paths and runs
# on any RISC-V target where -semihosting-config enable=on is honoured.
RT_C_VH_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello_vh
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# vh fixtures: crt0+trap+handler only (NO libgloss.c). -nodefaultlibs
# suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc; we re-add them
# in --start-group with -lgloss_vh inserted so newlib's syscall stubs
# resolve to the semihosting (virtual-hosting) implementations.
RT_C_VH_CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
RT_C_VH_RUNTIME := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler_vh.c
RT_C_VH_LIBS    := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets mstatus.VS
# to Initial (line 81), so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed separately so
# changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V from rt_c_v_*.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# vh fixtures: identical layout to RT_C_FIXTURES but link against
# -lgloss_vh instead of the custom runtime/libgloss.c.
$(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_VH_RUNTIME) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_VH_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RT_C_VH_RUNTIME) $< $(RT_C_VH_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_VH_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

toolUseResult.content:
/* Drain markers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
 *
 * Phase rule:
 *   The span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain() and rt_phase_vsim_drain()
 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall. Newlib stdio (printf,
 *   fputs, fwrite, ...) bottoms out in libgloss_vh's semihosting magic
 *   triple, which is intercepted by QEMU but not by vsim -- inside the
 *   span the triple would route to mtvec and loop. Phase 2 is restricted
 *   to register/memory work only.
 *
 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before rt_phase_qemu1_drain) and in
 *   QEMU2 (after rt_phase_vsim_drain) via standard newlib stdio. Both
 *   QEMU launches set -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native.
 */

#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
#define RT_C_HELPERS_H

/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
 *
 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
}
static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Drain markers and helpers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
 *
 * Phase rule (enforced by inspection):
 *   The span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain() and rt_phase_vsim_drain()
 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT perform any I/O -- no libgloss / sim_control
 *   MMIO (0xE0080000+), no RISC-V semihosting. vsim does not implement
 *   semihosting, and emitting status bytes during the cycle-accurate span
 *   pollutes the round-trip with non-architectural events.
 *
 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before rt_phase_qemu1_drain) and in
 *   QEMU2 (after rt_phase_vsim_drain) via rt_semihost_writes(). Both QEMU
 *   launches are configured with -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native
 *   (roundtrip_e2e.sh, rt_shared_mem.sh, qemu_handback.hpp).
 */

#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
#define RT_C_HELPERS_H

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
 *
 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
}
static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
}

/* RISC-V semihosting write-string. Safe in QEMU1 (phase 1) and QEMU2
 * (phase 3); both QEMUs are spawned with -semihosting-config
 * enable=on,target=native so the magic slli/ebreak/srai triple is
 * intercepted before reaching mtvec. MUST NOT be called inside the
 * vsim span (between rt_phase_qemu1_drain and rt_phase_vsim_drain):
 * vsim does not implement semihosting, the ebreak routes to
 * m_trap_entry -> exception_handler and loops. */
static inline void rt_semihost_writes(const char* s)
{
  register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x04; /* SYS_WRITE0 */
  register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long) (uintptr_t) s;
  __asm__ __volatile__(".option push\n"
                       ".option norvc\n"
                       "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\n"
                       "ebreak\n"
                       "srai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
                       ".option pop\n"
                       :
                       : "r"(a0), "r"(a1)
                       : "memory");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

/* Drain markers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
 *
 * Phase rule:
 *   The span between rt_phase_qemu1_drain() and rt_phase_vsim_drain()
 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall. Newlib stdio (printf,
 *   fputs, fwrite, ...) bottoms out in libgloss_vh's semihosting magic
 *   triple, which is intercepted by QEMU but not by vsim -- inside the
 *   span the triple would route to mtvec and loop. Phase 2 is restricted
 *   to register/memory work only.
 *
 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before rt_phase_qemu1_drain) and in
 *   QEMU2 (after rt_phase_vsim_drain) via standard newlib stdio. Both
 *   QEMU launches set -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native.
 */

#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
#define RT_C_HELPERS_H

/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
 *
 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
}
static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  rt_phase_qemu1_drain();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  rt_semihost_writes("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  return 0;
}

    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  rt_phase_vsim_drain();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        fflush(stdout);
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  fflush(stdout);
  return 0;
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via rt_semihost_writes(), matching the QEMU2 launch in
# qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from doing any I/O --
# sim_control MMIO and semihosting are both off-limits there.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via rt_semihost_writes(), matching the QEMU2 launch in
# qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from doing any I/O --
# sim_control MMIO and semihosting are both off-limits there.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

+# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.

1	/* Drain markers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
2	 *
3	 * Phase rule:
4	 *   The span between enter_vsim() and exit_vsim()
5	 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall. Newlib stdio (printf,
6	 *   fputs, fwrite, ...) bottoms out in libgloss_vh's semihosting magic
7	 *   triple, which is intercepted by QEMU but not by vsim -- inside the
8	 *   span the triple would route to mtvec and loop. Phase 2 is restricted
9	 *   to register/memory work only.
10	 *
11	 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before enter_vsim) and in
12	 *   QEMU2 (after exit_vsim) via standard newlib stdio. Both
13	 *   QEMU launches set -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native.
14	 */
15	
16	#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
17	#define RT_C_HELPERS_H
18	
19	/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
20	 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
21	 *
22	 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
23	 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
24	 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
25	 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
26	static inline void enter_vsim(void)
27	{
28	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
29	                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
30	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
31	}
32	static inline void exit_vsim(void)
33	{
34	  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
35	                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
36	                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
37	}
38	
39	#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

1	# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
2	# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...
3	
4	TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
5	CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
6	LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
7	OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump
8	
9	CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
10	LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static
11	
12	# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
13	RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
14	               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
15	               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival
16	
17	# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
18	# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
19	# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
20	# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
21	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
22	# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
23	# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
24	# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
25	RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
26	RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
27	# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
28	# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
29	PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
30	
31	RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
32	RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
33	                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
34	# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
35	# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
36	# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
37	# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
38	# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
39	RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
40	RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
41	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
42	# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
43	# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
44	RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
45	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
46	
47	.PHONY: all clean dump
48	
49	all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
50	     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
51	     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
52	     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)
53	
54	spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
55		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
56		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@
57	
58	handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
59		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
60		$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@
61	
62	handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
63		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
64		$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@
65	
66	handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
67		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
68		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@
69	
70	$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
71		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
72		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@
73	
74	# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
75	# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld
76	# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
77	$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
78		$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
79		  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@
80	
81	# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed
82	# separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V.
83	$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
84		$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
85		  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@
86	
87	# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
88	PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
89	$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
90		$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
91		$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@
92	
93	dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
94		$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
95		$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
96		$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf
97	
98	clean:
99		rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
100		      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
101		      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
102		      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
103		      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

1	/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
2	 *
3	 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
4	 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
5	 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
6	 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
7	 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
8	 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
9	 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
10	 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
11	 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
12	 *
13	 * V-state transport
14	 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
15	 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
16	 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
17	 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
18	 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
19	 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
20	 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
21	 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
22	 *   loading from the SEED region.
23	 *
24	 * Memory layout:
25	 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
26	 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
27	 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
28	 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
29	 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
30	 *
31	 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
32	 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
33	 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
34	 */
35	#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
36	
37	#include <stdint.h>
38	#include <stdio.h>
39	
40	#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
41	#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
42	#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */
43	
44	static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
45	static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;
46	
47	#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL
48	
49	static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
50	{
51	  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
52	    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
53	}
54	
55	int main(void)
56	{
57	  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
58	   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
59	   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
60	   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
61	   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
62	   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
63	   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
64	   * trigger. */
65	  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);
66	
67	  asm volatile(
68	    "li     a4, 16\n"
69	    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
70	    "mv     t0, %0\n"
71	    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
72	    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
73	    :: "r"(SEED)
74	    : "memory", "t0", "a4");
75	
76	  enter_vsim();
77	
78	  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
79	   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
80	   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
81	  asm volatile(
82	    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
83	    "li     a4, 16\n"
84	    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
85	    "mv     t0, %0\n"
86	    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
87	    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
88	    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
89	    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
90	    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
91	    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
92	    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
93	    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
94	    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
95	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
96	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
97	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
98	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
99	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
100	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
101	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
102	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
103	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
104	    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
105	    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
106	     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
107	    "li     a4, 64\n"
108	    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
109	    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
110	    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
111	    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
112	    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
113	    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
114	    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
115	    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
116	    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
117	    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
118	    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
119	    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Drain markers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
 *
 * Phase rule:
 *   The span between enter_vsim() and exit_vsim()
 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall. Newlib stdio (printf,
 *   fputs, fwrite, ...) bottoms out in libgloss_vh's semihosting magic
 *   triple, which is intercepted by QEMU but not by vsim -- inside the
 *   span the triple would route to mtvec and loop. Phase 2 is restricted
 *   to register/memory work only.
 *
 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before enter_vsim) and in
 *   QEMU2 (after exit_vsim) via standard newlib stdio. Both
 *   QEMU launches set -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native.
 */

#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
#define RT_C_HELPERS_H

/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
 *
 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
static inline void enter_vsim(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n");
}
static inline void exit_vsim(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

lines: * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
* the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
* each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
+/* The "memory" clobber forces the compiler to spill all globals to memory
+ * across the marker and reload them after, since vsim's drain+resume is
+ * (from the compiler's POV) an opaque event that could observe or alter
+ * any memory. Without it, the compiler may hoist global-pointer arithmetic
+ * (e.g. &_impure_ptr) into a callee-saved register across the marker and
+ * use it after resume; if vsim doesn't faithfully restore that register
+ * the post-resume code dereferences garbage. The clobber list deliberately
+ * does NOT include GPRs: the round-trip is required to preserve them. */

1	// Shared GDB register-name tables. Used by the QEMU TCG plugin (C) at
2	// drain time and by vsim's gdbstub handback client (C++) at restore
3	// time. The strings are the gdb wire contract for QEMU's RISC-V
4	// gdbstub - any reordering or renaming must be done in lock-step here.
5	
6	#ifndef HYBRID_REG_NAMES_H
7	#define HYBRID_REG_NAMES_H
8	
9	#ifdef __cplusplus
10	extern "C" {
11	#endif
12	
13	// Index = x register index. From riscv-64bit-cpu.xml.
14	static const char *const hybrid_gpr_names[32] = {
15	    "zero", "ra",  "sp",  "gp",  "tp",  "t0", "t1", "t2",
16	    "s0",   "s1",  "a0",  "a1",  "a2",  "a3", "a4", "a5",
17	    "a6",   "a7",  "s2",  "s3",  "s4",  "s5", "s6", "s7",
18	    "s8",   "s9",  "s10", "s11", "t3",  "t4", "t5", "t6",
19	};
20	
21	// Index = f register index. From riscv-64bit-fpu.xml.
22	static const char *const hybrid_fpr_names[32] = {
23	    "ft0", "ft1", "ft2",  "ft3",  "ft4", "ft5", "ft6", "ft7",
24	    "fs0", "fs1", "fa0",  "fa1",  "fa2", "fa3", "fa4", "fa5",
25	    "fa6", "fa7", "fs2",  "fs3",  "fs4", "fs5", "fs6", "fs7",
26	    "fs8", "fs9", "fs10", "fs11", "ft8", "ft9", "ft10","ft11",
27	};
28	
29	#ifdef __cplusplus
30	}  // extern "C"
31	#endif
32	
33	#endif  // HYBRID_REG_NAMES_H

content.text: Root cause: QEMU's gdb XML names x8 as `"fp"`, not `"s0"`. The plugin's `find_reg("s0")` returns NULL → `state.gpr[8] = 0` always. Fix:

content.content: The file /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/include/hybrid/reg_names.h has been updated successfully.

lines: -// Index = x register index. From riscv-64bit-cpu.xml.
+// Index = x register index. Matches the names in QEMU's
+// lookup). NB: x8 is "fp", NOT "s0" -- QEMU's gdbstub uses the
+// frame-pointer alias. Looking it up as "s0" yields a NULL handle and
+// silently zeros gpr[8] in the drained state.
