content.content:
1 # How the Hybrid Simulator Works 2 3 A friendly tour for engineers new to this codebase. 4 5 ## The Problem in One Paragraph 6 7 A pure RTL simulator (`vsim`, built on Verilator + SystemC) is **cycle-accurate**: it tells you exactly how many cycles every instruction takes inside the CPU pipeline. The price is speed: it runs at ~5-9 KHz. Booting Linux, running libc init, and warming up before reaching the actual code you care about can take hours. Meanwhile, QEMU runs the same program **functionally** at MHz speed, but it has no notion of pipeline cycles. 8 9 The hybrid simulator combines them: run the boring parts in QEMU, switch to vsim for the small region of interest (ROI) you actually want to measure, and (optionally) hand back to QEMU to finish. 10 11 ## High-Level Concept 12 13 Five big ideas make this possible: 14 15 1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant. 16 17 2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on. 18 19 3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff. 20 21 4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI. 22 23 5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording. 24 25 ## State Categories and Their Transport 26 27 The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose. 28 29 | Category | QEMU 1 -> snapshot | snapshot -> vsim hart | vsim hart -> snapshot | snapshot -> QEMU 2 | 30 |---------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| 31 | GPRs `x1..x31`, PC | TCG plugin reads | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC) | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read) | gdbstub `G` packet | 32 | `mtvec` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command | passthrough (not re-read) | gdbstub `P` | 33 | Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written) | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) | 34 | FPRs `f0..f31` | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) | 35 | PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr) | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (each slot) | 36 | V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl` | TCG plugin | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | 37 | V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | 38 | V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB) | TCG plugin | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) | 39 | `vlenb` | TCG plugin | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN) | passthrough (verified, not re-read) | not sent | 40 | Guest memory | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | 41 42 "Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2. 43 44 ## Trigger Modes 45 46 Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`. 47 48 | Mode | Entry into vsim | Exit out of vsim | Source of truth | 49 |------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------| 50 | CSR | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself | 51 | PC | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match) | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged | 52 53 Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`): 54 55 - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire. 56 - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT` - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC. 57 - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime. 58 59 ## Design Rationale 60 61 **One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2). 62 63 **QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`. 64 65 **Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. 66 67 **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. 68 69 **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. 70 71 **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. 72 73 **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. 74 75 ## Architecture Flowchart 76 77 ```mermaid 78 flowchart LR 79 subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] 80 SHM[("/dev/shm RAM
(mmap shared)
+ V scratch
at 0x80FF0000")] 81 STATE_Q["state_qemu.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] 82 STATE_V["state_vsim.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] 83 end 84 85 subgraph QEMU1["QEMU 1 (functional, MHz)"] 86 Q1_TCG["TCG insn loop"] 87 Q1_PLUGIN["hybrid_handoff.so
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,0
PC mode: enter_pc=0x..."] 88 Q1_TCG --> Q1_PLUGIN 89 end 90 91 subgraph VSIM["vsim (cycle-accurate, KHz)"] 92 V_PIPE["RTL pipeline
(Verilator + SystemC)"] 93 V_DM["JTAG DM driver (in-process)
Abstract Cmds + Program Buffer"] 94 V_TRIG["CsrTrigger
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,1 (or runtime form)
PC mode: --hybrid-exit-pc"] 95 V_CTRL["HandoffController
passthrough(snapshot) +
fresh GPR/PC/V-CSR drain"] 96 V_DM -.GPR/FPR/CSR + vsetvl.-> V_PIPE 97 V_PIPE -.retire signals.-> V_TRIG 98 V_TRIG --> V_CTRL 99 V_CTRL --> V_DM 100 end 101 102 subgraph QEMU2["QEMU 2 (functional, MHz)"] 103 Q2_GDB["gdbstub -gdb tcp::PORT -S"] 104 Q2_TCG["TCG insn loop"] 105 Q2_GDB -.G + per-name P packets.-> Q2_TCG 106 end 107 108 Q1_PLUGIN -- "drain ALL state" --> STATE_Q 109 Q1_PLUGIN -- "qemu_plugin_request_exit" --> VSIM 110 STATE_Q -- "--resume-from-qemu (passthrough seed)" --> V_CTRL 111 STATE_Q -- "ResumeDriver: GPR/PC/mtvec/V" --> V_DM 112 V_DM -.stage V regs.-> SHM 113 SHM -.drain V regs.-> V_DM 114 115 V_CTRL -- "merge fresh + passthrough" --> STATE_V 116 V_TRIG -- "exit code 200" --> QEMU2 117 STATE_V -- "RSP G + P packets" --> Q2_GDB 118 119 Q1_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM 120 V_PIPE <-->|"every load/store"| SHM 121 Q2_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM 122 ``` 123 124 ## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test 125 126 The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip. 127 128 Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: 129 130 - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. 131 - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. 132 133 ### What each phase does 134 135 ``` 136 Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) 137 ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- 138 seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) 139 csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches 140 vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) 141 csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 142 ``` 143 144 ### State that crosses each handoff 145 146 | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | 147 |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| 148 | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | 149 | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | 150 | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | 151 152 ### What each step proves 153 154 | Step | What it tests | 155 |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| 156 | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | 157 | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | 158 | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | 159 | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | 160 161 A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. 162 163 ## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For 164 165 You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists. 166 167 ### 1. The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase 168 169 | Phase | HTIF (`0xE008_xxxx`) | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak | What you can do | 170 |---------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| 171 | QEMU 1 | unmapped -> store-access-fault, infinite trap | not enabled at QEMU 1 launch | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no `fopen`, no `_exit`. | 172 | vsim | mapped (sim_control / display) | not implemented -> trap to `m_trap_entry` -> infinite trap | `printf`, `fopen`, full newlib via libgloss-HTIF | 173 | QEMU 2 | unmapped -> store-access-fault | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) | semihosting (`_exit`, `SYS_WRITE0`, etc). **No `printf`/`fopen`**. | 174 175 The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) makes the contract explicit: 176 177 ```c 178 // ===== QEMU 1 window ===== // pure regs/memory only 179 rt_phase_qemu1_drain(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 0 -> hand off to vsim 180 // ===== vsim window ===== // printf, fopen, libgloss MMIO are OK 181 rt_phase_vsim_drain(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 1 -> hand off to QEMU 2 182 // ===== QEMU 2 window ===== // semihosting OK; libgloss MMIO is NOT 183 ``` 184 185 If you call `printf` outside the vsim window, you trap. If you call semihosting (or fall into `_exit`) outside the QEMU 2 window, you trap. There is no third channel that works in all three; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.** 186 187 ### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation 188 189 vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely: 190 191 - Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost. 192 - Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost. 193 - Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost. 194 - Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins). 195 196 If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C. 197 198 ### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates 199 200 `v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes. 201 202 `rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example. 203 204 ### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains 205 206 ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop. 207 208 ### 5. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi` 209 210 The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it. 211 212 ### 6. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window 213 214 Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts: 215 216 - `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`). 217 - `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`). 218 - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code). 219 - Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`. 220 221 Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim. 222 223 ### 7. Microarchitectural state does not transfer 224 225 Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them. 226 227 ### Where each rule lives in the runtime 228 229 | Concern | File | 230 |-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| 231 | Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address) | `runtime/rt_c.ld` | 232 | `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear | `runtime/crt0.S` | 233 | M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` | 234 | HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`) | 235 | Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit` | 236 | Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...) | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` | 237 | `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) | 238 | QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | 239 240 For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`. 241 242 ## Known Limitations 243 244 These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list. 245 246 ### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped 247 248 **What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind. 249 250 **Affected registers:** 251 - All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`). 252 - 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped. 253 - All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`). 254 - `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target). 255 256 **Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP. 257 258 **Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase. 259 260 **Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block. 261 262 ### L2. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff 263 264 Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording. 265 266 ### L3. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs 267 268 The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug. 269 270 ### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined 271 272 Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. The HTIF address `0xE008xxxx` is the only MMIO that works in vsim, and it works there alone. 273 274 ### L5. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported 275 276 The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it. 277 278 ### L6. Single hart only (today) 279 280 The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end. 281 282 ## Where to Look Next 283 284 - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. 285 - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. 286 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. 287 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. 288 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. 289 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. 290 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. 291 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. 292 - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. 293 - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, libgloss.c, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap handler, newlib HTIF + semihosting stubs, phase-aware drain markers. 294 - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - per-fixture build rules; documents which `-march`/linker-script combination each tier uses. 295 - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. 296 297 ## Mental Model in One Sentence 298 299 > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2. 300

file.content:
# How the Hybrid Simulator Works A friendly tour for engineers new to this codebase. ## The Problem in One Paragraph A pure RTL simulator (`vsim`, built on Verilator + SystemC) is **cycle-accurate**: it tells you exactly how many cycles every instruction takes inside the CPU pipeline. The price is speed: it runs at ~5-9 KHz. Booting Linux, running libc init, and warming up before reaching the actual code you care about can take hours. Meanwhile, QEMU runs the same program **functionally** at MHz speed, but it has no notion of pipeline cycles. The hybrid simulator combines them: run the boring parts in QEMU, switch to vsim for the small region of interest (ROI) you actually want to measure, and (optionally) hand back to QEMU to finish. ## High-Level Concept Five big ideas make this possible: 1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant. 2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on. 3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff. 4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI. 5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording. ## State Categories and Their Transport The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose. | Category | QEMU 1 -> snapshot | snapshot -> vsim hart | vsim hart -> snapshot | snapshot -> QEMU 2 | |---------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| | GPRs `x1..x31`, PC | TCG plugin reads | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC) | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read) | gdbstub `G` packet | | `mtvec` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command | passthrough (not re-read) | gdbstub `P` | | Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written) | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) | | FPRs `f0..f31` | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) | | PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr) | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (each slot) | | V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl` | TCG plugin | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB) | TCG plugin | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) | | `vlenb` | TCG plugin | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN) | passthrough (verified, not re-read) | not sent | | Guest memory | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | "Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2. ## Trigger Modes Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`. | Mode | Entry into vsim | Exit out of vsim | Source of truth | |------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------| | CSR | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself | | PC | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match) | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged | Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`): - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT` - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime. ## Design Rationale **One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2). **QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`. **Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. ## Architecture Flowchart ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] SHM[("/dev/shm RAM
(mmap shared)
+ V scratch
at 0x80FF0000")] STATE_Q["state_qemu.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] STATE_V["state_vsim.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] end subgraph QEMU1["QEMU 1 (functional, MHz)"] Q1_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q1_PLUGIN["hybrid_handoff.so
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,0
PC mode: enter_pc=0x..."] Q1_TCG --> Q1_PLUGIN end subgraph VSIM["vsim (cycle-accurate, KHz)"] V_PIPE["RTL pipeline
(Verilator + SystemC)"] V_DM["JTAG DM driver (in-process)
Abstract Cmds + Program Buffer"] V_TRIG["CsrTrigger
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,1 (or runtime form)
PC mode: --hybrid-exit-pc"] V_CTRL["HandoffController
passthrough(snapshot) +
fresh GPR/PC/V-CSR drain"] V_DM -.GPR/FPR/CSR + vsetvl.-> V_PIPE V_PIPE -.retire signals.-> V_TRIG V_TRIG --> V_CTRL V_CTRL --> V_DM end subgraph QEMU2["QEMU 2 (functional, MHz)"] Q2_GDB["gdbstub -gdb tcp::PORT -S"] Q2_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q2_GDB -.G + per-name P packets.-> Q2_TCG end Q1_PLUGIN -- "drain ALL state" --> STATE_Q Q1_PLUGIN -- "qemu_plugin_request_exit" --> VSIM STATE_Q -- "--resume-from-qemu (passthrough seed)" --> V_CTRL STATE_Q -- "ResumeDriver: GPR/PC/mtvec/V" --> V_DM V_DM -.stage V regs.-> SHM SHM -.drain V regs.-> V_DM V_CTRL -- "merge fresh + passthrough" --> STATE_V V_TRIG -- "exit code 200" --> QEMU2 STATE_V -- "RSP G + P packets" --> Q2_GDB Q1_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM V_PIPE <-->|"every load/store"| SHM Q2_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM ``` ## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip. Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. ### What each phase does ``` Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 ``` ### State that crosses each handoff | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | ### What each step proves | Step | What it tests | |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. ## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists. ### 1. The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase | Phase | HTIF (`0xE008_xxxx`) | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak | What you can do | |---------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | QEMU 1 | unmapped -> store-access-fault, infinite trap | not enabled at QEMU 1 launch | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no `fopen`, no `_exit`. | | vsim | mapped (sim_control / display) | not implemented -> trap to `m_trap_entry` -> infinite trap | `printf`, `fopen`, full newlib via libgloss-HTIF | | QEMU 2 | unmapped -> store-access-fault | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) | semihosting (`_exit`, `SYS_WRITE0`, etc). **No `printf`/`fopen`**. | The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) makes the contract explicit: ```c // ===== QEMU 1 window ===== // pure regs/memory only rt_phase_qemu1_drain(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 0 -> hand off to vsim // ===== vsim window ===== // printf, fopen, libgloss MMIO are OK rt_phase_vsim_drain(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 1 -> hand off to QEMU 2 // ===== QEMU 2 window ===== // semihosting OK; libgloss MMIO is NOT ``` If you call `printf` outside the vsim window, you trap. If you call semihosting (or fall into `_exit`) outside the QEMU 2 window, you trap. There is no third channel that works in all three; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.** ### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely: - Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost. - Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost. - Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost. - Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins). If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C. ### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates `v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes. `rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example. ### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop. ### 5. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi` The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it. ### 6. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts: - `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`). - `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`). - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code). - Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`. Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim. ### 7. Microarchitectural state does not transfer Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them. ### Where each rule lives in the runtime | Concern | File | |-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address) | `runtime/rt_c.ld` | | `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear | `runtime/crt0.S` | | M-mode trap save/restore, ECALL dispatch | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` | | HTIF newlib stubs (`_open`/`_read`/`_write`/...) | `runtime/libgloss.c` (uses `0xE0080010`) | | Semihosting `_exit` (the `slli/ebreak/srai` triple) | `runtime/libgloss.c::_exit` | | Phase markers (`rt_phase_qemu1_drain`, ...) | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` | | `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) | | QEMU 2 launch flags (`-semihosting-config`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`. ## Known Limitations These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list. ### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped **What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind. **Affected registers:** - All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`). - 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped. - All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`). - `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target). **Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP. **Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase. **Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block. ### L2. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording. ### L3. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug. ### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. The HTIF address `0xE008xxxx` is the only MMIO that works in vsim, and it works there alone. ### L5. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it. ### L6. Single hart only (today) The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end. ## Where to Look Next - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, libgloss.c, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap handler, newlib HTIF + semihosting stubs, phase-aware drain markers. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - per-fixture build rules; documents which `-march`/linker-script combination each tier uses. - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. ## Mental Model in One Sentence > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2.

input.old_string:
### 1. The same MMIO addresses mean different things in each phase

| Phase   | HTIF (`0xE008_xxxx`)                          | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak                                   | What you can do                                              |
|---------|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| QEMU 1  | unmapped -> store-access-fault, infinite trap | not enabled at QEMU 1 launch                                       | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no `fopen`, no `_exit`. |
| vsim    | mapped (sim_control / display)                 | not implemented -> trap to `m_trap_entry` -> infinite trap        | `printf`, `fopen`, full newlib via libgloss-HTIF             |
| QEMU 2  | unmapped -> store-access-fault                 | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`)            | semihosting (`_exit`, `SYS_WRITE0`, etc). **No `printf`/`fopen`**. |

The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) makes the contract explicit:

```c
// ===== QEMU 1 window =====        // pure regs/memory only
rt_phase_qemu1_drain();             // csrwi 0x7C0, 0  -> hand off to vsim
// ===== vsim window =====          // printf, fopen, libgloss MMIO are OK
rt_phase_vsim_drain();              // csrwi 0x7C0, 1  -> hand off to QEMU 2
// ===== QEMU 2 window =====        // semihosting OK; libgloss MMIO is NOT
```

If you call `printf` outside the vsim window, you trap. If you call semihosting (or fall into `_exit`) outside the QEMU 2 window, you trap. There is no third channel that works in all three; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.**
input.new_string:
### 1. Phase 2 (vsim) must be syscall-free; phase 1 and 3 (QEMU) get standard newlib

C fixtures link against the Andes toolchain's virtual-hosting libgloss (`-lgloss_vh`), whose `_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/... all bottom out in the RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Both QEMU launches enable semihosting (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`), so the triple is intercepted before reaching `mtvec`. vsim does NOT implement semihosting -- inside the ROI the triple routes to `mtvec` -> `m_trap_entry` -> trap loop.

| Phase   | Standard newlib I/O (`printf`, `fopen`, ...) | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak                                  | What you can do                                              |
|---------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| QEMU 1  | works (via `-lgloss_vh` -> semihosting)      | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`)          | full newlib stdio: `printf`, `puts`, `fwrite`, etc.          |
| vsim    | TRAPS (newlib stdio -> `_write` -> semihosting ebreak -> `mtvec`) | not implemented -> trap loop                  | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no syscalls, no `_exit`. |
| QEMU 2  | works (same `-lgloss_vh` path)               | enabled                                                          | full newlib stdio.                                           |

The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) marks the boundary:

```c
// ===== QEMU 1 window =====        // printf etc. are fine
enter_vsim();                       // csrwi 0x7C0, 0  -> hand off to vsim
// ===== vsim window =====          // syscall-free: registers + memory only
exit_vsim();                        // csrwi 0x7C0, 1  -> hand off to QEMU 2
// ===== QEMU 2 window =====        // printf etc. are fine again
```

The forbidden-ness in phase 2 is transitive: anything that ultimately calls `_write`/`_exit`/`_open`/... (i.e. anything that emits via the libgloss syscall stubs) is out. Pure CPU functions are fine: `vsnprintf` into a buffer, `memcpy`, `<math.h>`, inline asm including V instructions, `setjmp`/`longjmp`. The pattern for cross-phase output is "compute in phase 2, emit in phase 1 or 3."

There is no third channel; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.**

Each rule is followed by the reason it exists.

| QEMU launch flags (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `tests/hybrid/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_shared_mem}.sh` (QEMU 1), `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (QEMU 2) |
| C-fixture build line (`-nodefaultlibs -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc`) | `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` |

lines: +| M-mode trap save/restore + fatal trap reporter    | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c`         |
+| Newlib syscall stubs (`_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/...) via semihosting | `-lgloss_vh` from the Andes toolchain |
| `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) |

For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`.

### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined

Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. The HTIF address `0xE008xxxx` is the only MMIO that works in vsim, and it works there alone.
input.new_string:
### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined

Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. vsim's only deterministic peripheral is shared memory (the `mmap` backing file); there is no console, no clock, no semihosting. Stick to register-and-memory work inside the ROI.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository Overview

Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz-level) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The system runs a three-phase round-trip: **QEMU1** (drain on CSR/PC trigger) -> **vsim** (resume + execute + drain) -> **QEMU2** (restore via gdbstub, continue).

Two Git submodules:
- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`)
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources; `hybrid/` subdirs hold the plugin and test fixtures

Shared ABI lives at `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (not inside either submodule).

## Build Commands

```sh
# Build everything (qemu -> vsim -> plugin)
source ./setup.sh

# Build individual components
./build_qemu.sh                              # QEMU (configure + make -j64)
./build_vsim.sh                              # vsim (Docker/podman, runs ./build.sh inside container)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./hybrid_qemu  # TCG plugin (out-of-tree)

# Build RISC-V ELF test fixtures
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test              # all fixtures
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test rt_c_v_regs.elf  # single fixture

# Clean
source ./setup.sh clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|all]
```

**Outputs:**
- `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (or other CPU variant)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`

`build_vsim.sh` is Docker-based (podman). It builds an image from `hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile` (target `env`) then runs `./build.sh` inside it, mounting the repo at `/work` and `include/` as `/include:ro`.

## Running Tests

```sh
# All e2e tests in parallel (auto-detects job count from nproc + /dev/shm headroom)
bash run_e2e.sh

# Run with specific options
JOBS=4 MODE=csr bash run_e2e.sh         # 4-way parallel, CSR-mode only
JOBS=1 bash run_e2e.sh                  # sequential (debug)
FILTER=rt_c_v bash run_e2e.sh           # regex filter on fixture name
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs bash run_e2e.sh    # custom log directory

# Unit tests (inside vsim build container)
# The container build runs: cmake --build build -j
# To run ctest directly after build:
#   podman run ... ./build.sh && podman exec ... ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

**Important:** on a high-core-count machine (128+ cores) `default_jobs()` computes `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` which can reach 128+. Running that many vsim Verilator processes simultaneously saturates CPU and causes timeouts. Use `JOBS=8` (or lower) as a practical cap: `JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh`.

`run_e2e.sh` exits 0 only when all tests pass. Logs go to a `mktemp` directory under `/tmp`; path is printed at the start of each run. Each test's full output lands in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`.

## Architecture

### Three-Phase Handoff

```
QEMU1 --(state file)--> vsim --(state file + V-scratch mmap)--> QEMU2
```

1. **QEMU plugin** (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`) - TCG plugin. On CSR-write trigger (`csrwi 0x7C0, 0`) or PC-trigger, drains QEMU's architectural state into a binary `hybrid_state_v1` struct written to `--outfile`.
2. **vsim handoff controller** (`src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`) - Detects the trigger inside vsim, pauses the hart via DMI, then calls `ResumeDriver` to restore state.
3. **ResumeDriver** (`src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`) - Writes GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-state into the vsim hart via RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands and Program Buffer.
4. **StateDrain** (`src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`) - On vsim exit trigger, reads back the full architectural state via DMI Abstract Commands.
5. **qemu_handback** (`src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`) - Spawns QEMU2, connects via gdbstub, sends P-packet register writes for GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs, then `continue`s.

### Trigger Modes

- **CSR mode** (default): Pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (entry) / `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) at retire time. CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp.
- **PC mode**: ELF symbols `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` resolved at startup; CSR insns are bypassed.

Both modes coexist in the fixtures. `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc` selects at runtime.

### Shared ABI (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)

`hybrid_state_v1` is the binary wire format exchanged between QEMU plugin, vsim, and the handback path. It holds: 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, etc.), PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg), and the V extension block (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, 32 vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr). `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64` supports VLEN up to 512 bits.

Flags (`HYBRID_FLAG_RV64`, `HYBRID_FLAG_FP`, `HYBRID_FLAG_V`) gate which state blocks are live. The ABI struct is validated by `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp`.

### V-State Transport

V register transport uses a fixed scratch region (`kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) in the shared mmap. vsim's inline asm writes all 32 V regs there via `vse8.v`; `StateDrain` reads those bytes into `hybrid_state_v1::vreg[]`. `qemu_handback` ships them to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets by register number.

V scalar CSR transport (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr):
- **Tier A** (Abstract Commands): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
- **Tier B** (Program Buffer): vtype + vl via `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (direct CSR write to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only; only `vsetvl` restores both atomically)

Restore order in `ResumeDriver::resume()` when `HYBRID_FLAG_V` is set:
1. `mstatus.VS` (enables V in the hart)
2. `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in Program Buffer (stages `vl` in x5, `vtype` in x6 via abstract GPR writes first)
3. `vstart` (AFTER vsetvl: vsetvl resets vstart to 0)
4. `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`
5. GPR loop (overwrites the x5/x6 scratch - safe now)

### Test Fixture Tiers

| Tier | Harness | Memory model | Examples |
|------|---------|-------------|---------|
| 1 | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` | QEMU-private RAM | handoff_roundtrip, rt_all_gprs, rt_csr_survival |
| 2 | `rt_shared_mem.sh` | shared `/dev/shm` mmap | rt_c_hello, rt_c_matmul, rt_c_v_regs, rt_c_v_csrs |

Tier-2 is required for C-level fixtures because the C stack and heap must survive the vsim -> QEMU2 boundary. The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`, then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.

Fixture source lives in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/`. C-level fixtures link against `runtime/` (crt0.S, trap.S, libgloss.c, handler.c) using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`.

### vsim CMake Structure

vsim lives entirely in `hybrid_vsim/`. Key CMake options:
- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (default OFF) - enables e2e CTest registration
- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default `config.yaml`)
- `VSIM_NO_VERILATOR` - skip Verilator build (assume prebuilt)

The hybrid orchestration layer (`src/hybrid/*.hpp`) is header-only and depends only on C++20 standard library + the shared ABI header. SystemC/Verilator are not required for hybrid unit tests.

Hybrid unit tests (`tests/hybrid/`) use [doctest](https://github.com/doctest/doctest). They are registered in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` and run via `ctest`. The `recording_bus.hpp` / `RecordingSession` patterns provide duck-typed test doubles for the DMI bus and gdbstub session without requiring virtual interfaces.

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | Wire-format struct (shared by all three components) |
| `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` | TCG plugin: drain on trigger |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` | Phase dispatcher |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` | DMI state restore (Tier A+B) |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` | DMI state capture |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` | Spawn QEMU2, gdbstub P-packets |
| `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` | V register transport via scratch region |
| `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` | CTest registration for all e2e fixtures |
| `run_e2e.sh` | Parallel e2e runner (FIFO job pool, per-test logs) |
| `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` | RISC-V ELF fixture build (Andes toolchain) |

content.content:
169:C fixtures link against the Andes toolchain's virtual-hosting libgloss (`-lgloss_vh`), whose `_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/... all bottom out in the RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Both QEMU launches enable semihosting (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`), so the triple is intercepted before reaching `mtvec`. vsim does NOT implement semihosting -- inside the ROI the triple routes to `mtvec` -> `m_trap_entry` -> trap loop.
181:enter_vsim();                       // csrwi 0x7C0, 0  -> hand off to vsim
183:exit_vsim();                        // csrwi 0x7C0, 1  -> hand off to QEMU 2
187:The forbidden-ness in phase 2 is transitive: anything that ultimately calls `_write`/`_exit`/`_open`/... (i.e. anything that emits via the libgloss syscall stubs) is out. Pure CPU functions are fine: `vsnprintf` into a buffer, `memcpy`, `<math.h>`, inline asm including V instructions, `setjmp`/`longjmp`. The pattern for cross-phase output is "compute in phase 2, emit in phase 1 or 3."
239:| Phase markers (`enter_vsim`, `exit_vsim`)         | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
297:- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap reporter, phase-aware drain markers. Newlib syscall stubs come from the toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting), not from a project-local libgloss.

Pure CPU functions are fine: `vsnprintf` into a buffer, `memcpy`, `<math.h>`, inline asm including V instructions, `setjmp`/`longjmp`. The pattern for cross-phase output is "compute in phase 2, emit in phase 1 or 3."
239:| Phase markers (`enter_vsim`, `exit_vsim`)         | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h`                       |
297:- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap reporter, phase-aware drain markers. Newlib syscall stubs come from the toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting), not from a project-local libgloss.
