file.content:
/* Drain markers shared by all C-level round-trip fixtures.
 *
 * Phase rule:
 *   The span between enter_vsim() and exit_vsim()
 *   runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall. Newlib stdio (printf,
 *   fputs, fwrite, ...) bottoms out in libgloss_vh's semihosting magic
 *   triple, which is intercepted by QEMU but not by vsim -- inside the
 *   span the triple would route to mtvec and loop. Phase 2 is restricted
 *   to register/memory work only.
 *
 *   Printing is permitted in QEMU1 (before enter_vsim) and in
 *   QEMU2 (after exit_vsim) via standard newlib stdio. Both
 *   QEMU launches set -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native.
 */

#ifndef RT_C_HELPERS_H
#define RT_C_HELPERS_H

/* Plugin- / handoff-controller-recognized drain markers (CSR 0x7C0,
 * uimm 0 = ENTER, uimm 1 = EXIT). Match insn_match.h.
 *
 * Each helper exposes a global `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` label co-located
 * with the csrwi insn. The labels are inert in CSR mode and consumed by
 * the PC-trigger mode driver (resolved via `nm --defined-only`). Because
 * each fixture calls each helper exactly once, the labels stay unique. */
/* The "memory" clobber forces the compiler to spill all globals to memory
 * across the marker and reload them after, since vsim's drain+resume is
 * (from the compiler's POV) an opaque event that could observe or alter
 * any memory. Without it, the compiler may hoist global-pointer arithmetic
 * (e.g. &_impure_ptr) into a callee-saved register across the marker and
 * use it after resume; if vsim doesn't faithfully restore that register
 * the post-resume code dereferences garbage. The clobber list deliberately
 * does NOT include GPRs: the round-trip is required to preserve them. */
static inline void enter_vsim(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_enter_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_enter_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 0\n" ::: "memory");
}
static inline void exit_vsim(void)
{
  __asm__ __volatile__(".globl _hybrid_exit_pc\n"
                       "_hybrid_exit_pc:\n"
                       "csrwi 0x7C0, 1\n" ::: "memory");
}

#endif /* RT_C_HELPERS_H */

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed
# separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  enter_vsim();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  exit_vsim();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        fflush(stdout);
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  fflush(stdout);
  return 0;
}

content.text:
Short answer: static and malloc'd buffers both fail because every phase boundary re-loads the ELF.

The PT_LOAD segments cover `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` up to `_end` (~0x8000d800 per `rt_c.ld:35`). On each phase entry:

- **QEMU1 `-kernel`**: loads ELF, writes file contents into `.text/.rodata/.data`, zero-fills `.bss`.
- **vsim** at `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp:95`: same - copies `filesz` then `std::fill_n(... memsz - filesz, 0)` zero-fills the bss tail.
- **QEMU2 `-kernel`**: same as QEMU1.

So:

- **`static int32_t A[256]`** lands in `.bss` -> zeroed by vsim's loader at handoff, again by QEMU2 at the second handoff. QEMU1's seed values vanish before `vmatmul_kernel` runs.
- **`malloc()`** returns memory past `_end`, which IS outside any PT_LOAD's memsz, so the bytes do survive. But:
  - The libc brk pointer lives in `.data/.bss` and gets reset on every ELF reload, so the heap accountant resets each phase.
  - The pointer itself must survive across `enter_vsim/exit_vsim`. A file-scope `static int32_t *A` would be in `.bss` (zeroed) or `.data` (reloaded to its initial value). Only a function-local would survive (the `"memory"` clobber forces a stack spill, and the stack is in the shared mmap with SP restored). Workable but indirect.

Fixed addresses at `0x80100000+` sit above `_end` and below `_stack=0x80700000`, outside any PT_LOAD, so no loader touches them. The `static int32_t* const` pointers themselves live in `.rodata`, which is reloaded from disk identically each time - so the pointer value is stable and the data is preserved by the shared `memory-backend-file` mmap.

message.content: what is PT_LOAD?

content.text:
`PT_LOAD` is an ELF program-header type meaning "load this segment into memory at runtime." Each PT_LOAD entry tells the loader:

- `p_offset` / `p_filesz`: where in the file the bytes come from, and how many to copy.
- `p_vaddr` / `p_paddr`: target virtual/physical address in memory.
- `p_memsz`: how much memory to reserve. If `memsz > filesz`, the loader zero-fills the tail (this is how `.bss` works - it has memsz but zero filesz).
- `p_flags`: R/W/X permissions.

An ELF typically has 2-3 PT_LOAD segments (e.g. one R+X for `.text/.rodata`, one R+W for `.data/.bss`). The loader walks the program header table, finds each PT_LOAD, copies/zero-fills accordingly, and ignores other types (`PT_NOTE`, `PT_DYNAMIC`, etc.).

Inspect with `riscv64-unknown-elf-readelf -l rt_c_v_matmul.elf` - look for the "Program Headers" table.

content.text:
The mmap is fine. The destroyer is the **redundant ELF re-load at each phase**, not the mmap.

Three reload sites overwrite the shared file:

1. **QEMU1 `-kernel`**: writes `.text/.rodata/.data` from disk, zero-fills `.bss` (memsz > filesz). Necessary - this is what populates the shared mmap initially.
2. **vsim** `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp:93-95`: `std::copy_n(filesz)` + `std::fill_n(memsz - filesz, 0)`. Wipes phase-1 writes to `.bss` and resets `.data` to ELF initial values. **Redundant** - the bytes are already in the shared mmap.
3. **QEMU2** `qemu_handback.hpp:153-154`: same as QEMU1. **Also redundant** - vsim already left the bytes in the shared mmap.

With static buffers in `.bss`: QEMU1's writes get nuked by site 2 before vsim phase 2 ever runs. With malloc: the data past `_end` actually survives (outside any PT_LOAD's memsz), but newlib's brk cursor lives in `.data/.bss` and resets, so the allocator forgets its own bookkeeping.

**Architectural fix** - drop sites 2 and 3:

- **vsim**: skip `read_elf_into_memory` in hybrid resume mode. Memory is populated by QEMU1's `-kernel` already; vsim's resume PC comes from the state file. The ELF path is still passed in for *symbol* lookup (PC-mode triggers via `nm`) - just not loaded into RAM.
- **QEMU2**: drop the `-kernel <elf>` pair from `spawn_qemu`. With `-bios none -S` and the shared memory backend, QEMU starts halted with code/data already in memory; the gdbstub P-packet sets PC, `c` resumes.

After that, `.bss`, `.data`, and malloc-heap all survive across phases. Users write normal C; the fixed-address contortion goes away.

Want me to implement this? It's two small edits but needs to be guarded so non-hybrid vsim runs still load the ELF.

99	#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.

103	# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.

SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime, different -march. Listed
# separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)

  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_qemu_handback tests/hybrid/qemu_handback.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_qemu_handback PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_qemu_handback)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # RVV register survival: seeds v8..v15 in QEMU1, mutates v9 in vsim,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Requires vsim-side V transport (v_regfile_xfer +
    # resume_driver / state_drain wiring).
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p))
total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f))
total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s))
step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

1	// rt_shared_mem.S
2	//
3	// Tier-2 fixture: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
4	// Unlike Tier-1 fixtures, all three phases see the same backing file via
5	// QEMU's `-object memory-backend-file,share=on` and vsim's
6	// `--shared-mem-path`. Memory written in Phase A is visible in Phase B; the
7	// page Phase B mutates is visible in Phase C.
8	//
9	// Phase A (QEMU1, shared mmap): fill 4 KB at 0x80010000 with 0xA5A5..A5.
10	// Phase B (vsim, same mmap):
11	//   1. read back; first mismatch -> exit 50.
12	//   2. XOR every word with 0x5A5A..5A in place; result is all-FF.
13	// Phase C (QEMU2, same mmap):
14	//   1. verify all 4 KB == 0xFF; first mismatch -> exit 51.
15	//   2. exit 0 on PASS.
16	//
17	// Distinct exit codes (50, 51) avoid the silent-pass aliasing that a
18	// running mismatch counter would have when (count mod 256) lands on 0.
19	
20	#include "semihost_exit.inc"
21	
22	.section .text
23	.globl _start
24	_start:
25	    // Phase A: fill 512 doublewords at 0x80010000 with 0xA5A5...A5.
26	    li      t0, 0x80010000
27	    li      t3, 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5
28	    li      t1, 512
29	1:
30	    sd      t3, 0(t0)
31	    addi    t0, t0, 8
32	    addi    t1, t1, -1
33	    bnez    t1, 1b
34	
35	.globl _hybrid_enter_pc
36	_hybrid_enter_pc:
37	    csrwi   0x7C0, 0                        // QEMU1 drains, exits
38	
39	    // Phase B: verify Phase A pattern, XOR with 0x5A...5A in place.
40	    li      t0, 0x80010000
41	    li      t3, 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5
42	    li      t4, 0x5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5A
43	    li      t1, 512
44	verify_a:
45	    ld      t2, 0(t0)
46	    bne     t2, t3, fail_b
47	    addi    t0, t0, 8
48	    addi    t1, t1, -1
49	    bnez    t1, verify_a
50	
51	    li      t0, 0x80010000
52	    li      t1, 512
53	xor_loop:
54	    ld      t2, 0(t0)
55	    xor     t2, t2, t4
56	    sd      t2, 0(t0)
57	    addi    t0, t0, 8
58	    addi    t1, t1, -1
59	    bnez    t1, xor_loop
60	
61	.globl _hybrid_exit_pc
62	_hybrid_exit_pc:
63	    csrwi   0x7C0, 1                        // vsim drains, exits 200
64	
65	phase_c:
66	    // Phase C: verify all 4 KB == 0xFFFF...FF through the shared mmap.
67	    li      t0, 0x80010000
68	    li      t5, -1                          // sign-extends to 0xFFFF...FF
69	    li      t1, 512
70	verify_c:
71	    ld      t2, 0(t0)
72	    bne     t2, t5, fail_c
73	    addi    t0, t0, 8
74	    addi    t1, t1, -1
75	    bnez    t1, verify_c
76	
77	    li      a2, 0
78	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
79	
80	fail_b:
81	    li      a2, 50
82	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
83	
84	fail_c:
85	    li      a2, 51
86	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
87

// rt_shared_mem.S
//
// Tier-2 fixture: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
// Unlike Tier-1 fixtures, all three phases see the same backing file via
// QEMU's `-object memory-backend-file,share=on` and vsim's
// `--shared-mem-path`. Memory written in Phase A is visible in Phase B; the
// page Phase B mutates is visible in Phase C.
//
// Phase A (QEMU1, shared mmap): fill 4 KB at 0x80010000 with 0xA5A5..A5.
// Phase B (vsim, same mmap):
//   1. read back; first mismatch -> exit 50.
//   2. XOR every word with 0x5A5A..5A in place; result is all-FF.
// Phase C (QEMU2, same mmap):
//   1. verify all 4 KB == 0xFF; first mismatch -> exit 51.
//   2. exit 0 on PASS.
//
// Distinct exit codes (50, 51) avoid the silent-pass aliasing that a
// running mismatch counter would have when (count mod 256) lands on 0.

#include "semihost_exit.inc"

.section .text
.globl _start
_start:
    // Phase A: fill 512 doublewords at 0x80010000 with 0xA5A5...A5.
    li      t0, 0x80010000
    li      t3, 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5
    li      t1, 512
1:
    sd      t3, 0(t0)
    addi    t0, t0, 8
    addi    t1, t1, -1
    bnez    t1, 1b

.globl _hybrid_enter_pc
_hybrid_enter_pc:
    csrwi   0x7C0, 0                        // QEMU1 drains, exits

    // Phase B: verify Phase A pattern, XOR with 0x5A...5A in place.
    li      t0, 0x80010000
    li      t3, 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5
    li      t4, 0x5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5A
    li      t1, 512
verify_a:
    ld      t2, 0(t0)
    bne     t2, t3, fail_b
    addi    t0, t0, 8
    addi    t1, t1, -1
    bnez    t1, verify_a

    li      t0, 0x80010000
    li      t1, 512
xor_loop:
    ld      t2, 0(t0)
    xor     t2, t2, t4
    sd      t2, 0(t0)
    addi    t0, t0, 8
    addi    t1, t1, -1
    bnez    t1, xor_loop

.globl _hybrid_exit_pc
_hybrid_exit_pc:
    csrwi   0x7C0, 1                        // vsim drains, exits 200

phase_c:
    // Phase C: verify all 4 KB == 0xFFFF...FF through the shared mmap.
    li      t0, 0x80010000
    li      t5, -1                          // sign-extends to 0xFFFF...FF
    li      t1, 512
verify_c:
    ld      t2, 0(t0)
    bne     t2, t5, fail_c
    addi    t0, t0, 8
    addi    t1, t1, -1
    bnez    t1, verify_c

    li      a2, 0
    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2

fail_b:
    li      a2, 50
    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2

fail_c:
    li      a2, 51
    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
3	#
4	# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
5	# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
6	# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
7	#
8	# Required environment:
9	#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
10	#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
11	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so
12	
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
16	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
17	
18	# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
19	# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
20	# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
21	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
22	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
23	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
24	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
25	
26	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
27	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
28	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
29	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
30	NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
31	
32	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
33	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
34	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
35	    exit 77
36	  fi
37	done
38	
39	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
40	  csr) ;;
41	  pc)
42	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
43	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
44	      exit 77
45	    fi
46	    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
47	    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
48	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
49	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
50	               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
51	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
52	              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
53	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
54	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
55	      exit 1
56	    fi
57	    ;;
58	  *)
59	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
60	    exit 1
61	    ;;
62	esac
63	
64	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
65	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
66	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
67	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
68	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
69	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
70	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
71	
72	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
73	
74	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
75	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
76	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
77	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
78	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
79	fi
80	
81	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
82	# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
83	# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
84	# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
85	# under either VLEN.
86	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
87	# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
88	# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
89	# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
90	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
91	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
92	  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
93	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
94	  -bios none -nographic \
95	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
96	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
97	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
98	
99	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
100	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
101	  exit 1
102	fi
103	
104	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
105	set +e
106	timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
107	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
108	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
109	  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
110	  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
111	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
112	  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
113	RC=$?
114	set -e
115	
116	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
117	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
118	  echo "--- sim log ---"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

content.text:
The issue is clear: skipping load_elf leaves reset_vector at 0, so the hart hits bus errors at PC=0 before the DMI halt/resume sequence finishes. rt_c_static_buf masked this because its phase 2 is a single instruction.

Fix: still set the reset vector from the ELF entry; just skip the memory copy.

- `hybrid_vsim/src/simulator.hpp:267-289` - `load_elf` now takes `skip_memory_load=false`. When set, it parses the ELF and writes the reset vector but skips `read_elf_into_memory`. The reset vector still needs to point to a valid PC because between `sim.reset()` and the DMI halt+DPC-write in `apply_resume_from_qemu`, the hart fetches one or two instructions; PC=0 hits unmapped memory and corrupts the resume.
- `hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:406-414` - gates `skip_memory_load=true` on `(--resume-from-qemu && --shared-mem-path)`, i.e. tier-2 hybrid resume.
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:151-160` - drops `-kernel <elf>` from QEMU 2's argv when `shared_mem_path` is set. The shared mmap already has code/data; gdbstub P32 sets PC. Tier-1 (no shared mmap) still passes `-kernel`.

**Tests**
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_static_buf.c` (new) - `static uint32_t buf[64]` seeded in QEMU 1, verified in QEMU 2. Phase 2 is a no-op.
- Wired into `Makefile`, `HybridConfig.cmake`, and `run_e2e.sh`.

**Result:** `JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh` -> 38 passed, 0 failed (16 existing csr + 1 new csr + 16 existing pc + 1 new pc + 4 negative).

User code in tier-2 fixtures can now use `static` and (with proper care for the brk pointer which is also in `.bss`) `malloc`. The fixed-address pattern in `rt_c_matmul.c` / `rt_c_v_matmul.c` is no longer required - want me to migrate them as a follow-up?

message.content: yes migrate them

1	/* rt_c_v_csrs.c - Round-trip fixture: V scalar CSR survival.
2	 *
3	 * Exercises vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vtype, vl, and vstart across the
4	 * QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 handoff.
5	 *
6	 * Phase 1 (QEMU1): drain marker only (no V instructions; plugin PC
7	 *   drain constraint).
8	 * Phase 2 (vsim):  configure known V CSR values, then drain.
9	 *   - vsetvli: vl=8, vtype=e32/m1/ta/ma (0xD0)
10	 *   - csrw vxsat=1, vxrm=2, vstart=3 (vstart last: vsetvli resets it to 0)
11	 * Phase 3 (QEMU2): read V CSRs via csrr and verify each expected value.
12	 *   mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no extra csrs needed.
13	 *   vstart is read before any V instruction so it holds the restored value.
14	 *
15	 * vcsr[0] = vxsat, vcsr[2:1] = vxrm -> expected vcsr = (2<<1)|1 = 5.
16	 * vtype: vsew=e32=010 at bits[5:3], vta=1 at bit6, vma=1 at bit7 -> 0xD0.
17	 */
18	#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
19	
20	#include <stdint.h>
21	#include <stdio.h>
22	
23	int main(void)
24	{
25	  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): no V instructions before the drain marker. */
26	  enter_vsim();
27	
28	  /* Phase 2 (vsim): set known V CSR values.
29	   * crt0.S already set mstatus.VS=non-Off so vsetvli doesn't trap.
30	   * vstart must be written last: vsetvli resets vstart to 0. */
31	  asm volatile(
32	    "li     a0, 8\n"
33	    "vsetvli t0, a0, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"  /* vl=8, vtype=0xD0 */
34	    "li     t1, 1\n"
35	    "csrw   vxsat, t1\n"
36	    "li     t1, 2\n"
37	    "csrw   vxrm,  t1\n"
38	    "li     t1, 3\n"
39	    "csrw   vstart, t1\n"
40	    ::: "t0", "t1", "a0", "memory");
41	
42	  exit_vsim();
43	
44	  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): verify V CSRs survived the round-trip.
45	   * Read vstart first, before any V instruction resets it to 0. */
46	  uint64_t vstart_out, vxsat_out, vxrm_out, vcsr_out, vtype_out, vl_out;
47	  asm volatile(
48	    "csrr %0, vstart\n"
49	    "csrr %1, vxsat\n"
50	    "csrr %2, vxrm\n"
51	    "csrr %3, vcsr\n"
52	    "csrr %4, vtype\n"
53	    "csrr %5, vl\n"
54	    : "=r"(vstart_out), "=r"(vxsat_out), "=r"(vxrm_out),
55	      "=r"(vcsr_out),   "=r"(vtype_out), "=r"(vl_out));
56	
57	  int ok = 1;
58	  if (vl_out     != 8u)    { printf("FAIL vl\n");     ok = 0; }
59	  if (vxsat_out  != 1u)    { printf("FAIL vxsat\n");  ok = 0; }
60	  if (vxrm_out   != 2u)    { printf("FAIL vxrm\n");   ok = 0; }
61	  if (vcsr_out   != 5u)    { printf("FAIL vcsr\n");   ok = 0; }
62	  if (vtype_out  != 0xD0u) { printf("FAIL vtype\n");  ok = 0; }
63	  if (vstart_out != 3u)    { printf("FAIL vstart\n"); ok = 0; }
64	
65	  printf("rt_c_v_csrs: %s\n", ok ? "PASS" : "FAIL");
66	  fflush(stdout);
67	  return ok ? 0 : 1;
68	}
69

String: /* Tier-2 C-level fixture: 16x16x16 GEMM split across all three phases.
 *
 *   QEMU 1: init_matrices -- fills A and B with deterministic patterns.
 *   vsim:   matmul_kernel -- pure C = A * B, no syscalls (phase-2 rule).
 *   QEMU 2: prints C row-by-row via printf, then re-derives the expected
 *           value of every C[i][j] and compares. Mismatch -> _exit(1).
 *
 * Cross-phase memory survival
 *   vsim's elf loader (utils/elf_loader.hpp:94) zero-fills (memsz - filesz)
 *   for every PT_LOAD on resume, which would blow away anything in .bss
 *   that QEMU 1 had written. The matrices therefore live at fixed addresses
 *   (0x80100000+) -- inside the 128 MiB shared mmap, above the ELF's last
 *   loaded byte (~0x8000d800), below the stack (0x80700000), so QEMU's
 *   -kernel load and vsim's loader both leave them untouched. The shared
 *   memory-backend-file then preserves the bytes across QEMU 1 -> vsim ->
 *   QEMU2.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define N 16

/* Fixed-address scratch region. Pointers (not arrays) so the compiler
 * doesn't reserve .bss for them. */
static int32_t* const A = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const B = (int32_t*) 0x80101000UL;
static int32_t* const C = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;</tool_use_error>

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  enter_vsim();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  exit_vsim();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        fflush(stdout);
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  fflush(stdout);
  return 0;
}

`rt_c_v_csrs.c` had no fixed-address buffers - V CSRs are register state, nothing to migrate.

@@ -1,21 +1,15 @@
 /* Tier-2 C-level fixture: 16x16x16 GEMM split across all three phases.
  *
  *   QEMU 1: init_matrices -- fills A and B with deterministic patterns.
- *   vsim:   matmul_kernel -- the only phase that touches sim_control via
- *           printf(); also computes C = A * B.
- *   QEMU 2: prints C row-by-row via RISC-V semihosting (SYS_WRITE0,
- *           QEMU virt has no sim_control), then re-derives the expected
+ *   vsim:   matmul_kernel -- pure C = A * B, no syscalls (phase-2 rule).
+ *   QEMU 2: prints C row-by-row via printf, then re-derives the expected
  *           value of every C[i][j] and compares. Mismatch -> _exit(1).
  *
  * Cross-phase memory survival
- *   vsim's elf loader (utils/elf_loader.hpp:94) zero-fills (memsz - filesz)
- *   for every PT_LOAD on resume, which would blow away anything in .bss
- *   that QEMU 1 had written. The matrices therefore live at fixed addresses
- *   (0x80100000+) -- inside the 128 MiB shared mmap, above the ELF's last
- *   loaded byte (~0x8000d800), below the stack (0x80700000), so QEMU's
- *   -kernel load and vsim's loader both leave them untouched. The shared
- *   memory-backend-file then preserves the bytes across QEMU1 -> vsim ->
- *   QEMU2.
+ *   Matrices sit in .bss. The "no redundant ELF reload" rule (vsim
+ *   skips load_elf and QEMU 2 drops -kernel when --shared-mem-path is
+ *   set) keeps phase-1 .bss writes intact through the shared
+ *   memory-backend-file.
  */

input.old_string:
* Memory layout:
 *   SEED / PHASE_C_OUT       - .bss, preserved by the "no redundant ELF
 *                              reload" rule (vsim skips load_elf and
 *                              QEMU 2 drops -kernel under shared mmap).
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000  - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                              64 bytes = 2048 B). Fixed by the ABI
 *                              between vsim's V transport and this
 *                              fixture's vsim-phase asm; cannot move.
 *   stack at 0x80700000      - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
