toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED / PHASE_C_OUT       - .bss, preserved by the "no redundant ELF
 *                              reload" rule (vsim skips load_elf and
 *                              QEMU 2 drops -kernel under shared mmap).
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000  - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                              64 bytes = 2048 B). Fixed by the ABI
 *                              between vsim's V transport and this
 *                              fixture's vsim-phase asm; cannot move.
 *   stack at 0x80700000      - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t SEED[VL_E32];
static int32_t PHASE_C_OUT[NREGS * VL_E32];

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  enter_vsim();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  exit_vsim();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        fflush(stdout);
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  fflush(stdout);
  return 0;
}

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: tier-2 C-fixture path; skip redundant ELF reload

- Link C fixtures against the Andes toolchain's libgloss_vh.a so
  newlib stdio bottoms out in RISC-V semihosting; remove the
  project-specific libgloss/htif/syscall stubs and the unused
  ecall syscall-dispatch table in runtime/handler.c.
- Establish the phase-2 rule: the span between enter_vsim() and
  exit_vsim() runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (vsim
  does not implement semihosting). Drain markers renamed from
  rt_phase_qemu1_drain/rt_phase_vsim_drain to enter_vsim/exit_vsim.
  QEMU 1 and QEMU 2 launches gain -semihosting-config target=native
  to intercept the magic triple before mtvec.
- In tier-2 hybrid resume (--shared-mem-path + --resume-from-qemu),
  vsim skips read_elf_into_memory and QEMU 2 drops -kernel: the
  shared memory-backend-file already holds the program from QEMU 1's
  initial -kernel load, and re-loading would std::fill_n the .bss
  tail of each PT_LOAD over phase-1's writes. vsim still parses the
  ELF to set the reset vector (the hart fetches a few instructions
  between sim.reset() and the DMI halt; an unmapped PC there leaves
  the hart in an unrecoverable state).
- Add rt_c_static_buf regression: a static uint32_t buf[64] seeded
  in phase 1, no-op in phase 2, verified in phase 3. Fails on the
  HEAD code path; passes with the skip-reload fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED at 0x80100000        - input pattern (16 int32, 64 bytes)
 *   PHASE_C_OUT at 0x80102000 - QEMU2 verify scratch (8 regs * 64 = 512 B)
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000   - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                                64 bytes = 2048 B)
 *   stack at 0x80700000       - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t* const SEED        = (int32_t*) 0x80100000UL;
static int32_t* const PHASE_C_OUT = (int32_t*) 0x80102000UL;

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  enter_vsim();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  exit_vsim();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        fflush(stdout);
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  fflush(stdout);
  return 0;
}

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
structural: migrate tier-2 fixtures from fixed-address pointers to static buffers

A/B/C in rt_c_matmul and rt_c_v_matmul, SEED/PHASE_C_OUT in
rt_c_v_regs: switch from fixed-address `int32_t* const X = (int32_t*)
0x801xxxx` pointers to plain `static int32_t X[N]` arrays. The
preceding "no redundant ELF reload" fix preserves .bss across the
hybrid round-trip, so user code can use static storage normally.

V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000 in rt_c_v_regs stays fixed: that address is
the ABI contract with vsim's V transport (hybrid::kVScratchGpa),
not user-allocatable.

No behavior change; existing CSR+PC e2e runs continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

input.command:
git add include/hybrid/reg_names.h && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: gdbstub register table uses "fp" for x8

QEMU's gdb-xml/riscv-64bit-cpu.xml advertises x8 under the
frame-pointer alias, not "s0". Looking it up as "s0" via
qemu_plugin_register_lookup returns NULL, which the plugin's drain
path silently treated as "this register is unavailable" and wrote
gpr[8]=0 into the state file. Round-trips that depended on the
compiler allocating &_impure_ptr (gp+808) into s0 across the vsim
boundary then dereferenced 0 in QEMU 2 and load-faulted at the
post-resume PC.

The library shape (single shared table, indexed by x register
number) is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

My expectation is user don't need to change their code and could use static buffer and malloc as usual." (with /effort max)
   
   d. **Implement the architectural fix**: "yes, implement it" - drop ELF reload in vsim (skip read_elf_into_memory in tier-2 resume) and QEMU2 (drop -kernel when shared mmap is in use)
   
   e. **Migrate existing fixtures**: "yes migrate them" - move rt_c_matmul, rt_c_v_matmul, rt_c_v_regs from fixed-address pointers to static buffers
   
   f. **Commit**: "commit this"
   
   g. **Push**: "push it"

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU + vsim sharing state via magic-CSR boundary
   - Three-phase round-trip: QEMU1 (drain) -> vsim (resume + execute + drain) -> QEMU2 (restore via gdbstub)
   - ELF PT_LOAD segments: filesz vs memsz, std::fill_n zero-fills (memsz - filesz)
   - .bss/.data clobbering by redundant ELF re-load at each phase
   - Shared memory-backend-file via `/dev/shm` for cross-phase memory survival
   - RISC-V Debug Module: DMI Abstract Commands, Program Buffer, DPC, haltreq, resumereq
   - gdbstub P-packets for register restore in QEMU2
   - Andes libgloss_vh.a (virtual-hosting via semihosting magic triple)
   - Tier-1 (private RAM) vs Tier-2 (shared mmap) fixtures
   - Reset vector vs DPC: hart fetches a few cycles from reset_vector before DMI halt takes effect
   - TDD: RED -> GREEN -> Refactor; Tidy First (structural vs behavioral commits)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_static_buf.c`** (CREATED)
     - New regression fixture: static uint32_t buf[64] in .bss, seeded in phase 1, no-op phase 2, verified in phase 3. Demonstrates the bug pre-fix.
     ```c
     #include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
     #include <stdint.h>
     #include <stdio.h>
     #define N 64
     static uint32_t buf[N];
     static uint32_t pattern(int i) {
       return (uint32_t)((uint32_t) i * 0xDEADBEEFu + 0x12345678u);
     }
     int main(void) {
       for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) buf[i] = pattern(i);
       enter_vsim();
       exit_vsim();
       for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
         const uint32_t exp = pattern(i);
         if (buf[i] != exp) { printf("rt_c_static_buf: FAIL ..."); fflush(stdout); return 1; }
       }
       printf("rt_c_static_buf: PASS\n"); fflush(stdout); return 0;
     }
     ```
   
   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/simulator.hpp:267-289`** (MODIFIED)
     - Added `skip_memory_load=false` parameter to `load_elf`. When true, parses ELF and sets reset vector but skips `read_elf_into_memory`.
     ```cpp
     void load_elf(const std::string& path, bool skip_memory_load = false) {
       const auto elf = LIEF::ELF::Parser::parse(path);
       if (!elf) { spdlog::critical(...); exit(1); }
       if (!skip_memory_load)
         read_elf_into_memory(*elf, ..., platform.main_memory.data);
       for (auto& rv: platform.reset_vector)
         rv.write(elf->entrypoint());
     }
     ```
   
   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:406-414`** (MODIFIED)
     - Gates `skip_memory_load=true` on tier-2 hybrid resume.
     ```cpp
     const bool elf_already_in_shared_mmap =
       !args.resume_from_qemu.empty() && !args.shared_mem_path.empty();
     if (!args.elf_file.empty() && !args.continue_ctx)
       sim.load_elf(args.elf_file, /*skip_memory_load=*/elf_already_in_shared_mmap);
     ```
   
   - **`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:151-160`** (MODIFIED)
     - Drops `-kernel <elf>` from QEMU2 when shared mmap is in use.
     ```cpp
     if (shared_mem_path.empty()) {
       argv.push_back("-kernel");
       argv.push_back(elf.c_str());
     }
     ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   
   - **First failed e2e run after fix**: `rt_shared_mem[csr]` and `rt_shared_mem[pc]` failed with `target socket not found for address 0x0/0x8` flood and `[info] hart 0 reset vector: 0x0`.
     - **Root cause**: Skipping `sim.load_elf()` left reset_vector at 0. Between `sim.reset()` and the DMI halt in `apply_resume_from_qemu`, the hart fetches a few cycles from reset_vector. PC=0 hits unmapped memory and the bus errors corrupt the hart state, even though apply_resume_from_qemu eventually writes DPC.

   - **First push attempt failed**: "src refspec hybrid does not match any" because the working directory had shifted to the parent repo (`/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim`) but I was trying to push submodule's branch.

file.content:
/* Tier-2 C-level round-trip: RVV register survival across the handoff.
 *
 *   QEMU1: seed an int32 pattern in shared memory and exercise a small V
 *          load+vadd to cover the case of V instructions retiring just
 *          before the drain marker (regression coverage for the plugin's
 *          translation-time HandoffSite PC capture).
 *   vsim:  vle32.v v8..v15 from the seed; vadd.vi v9 += 100; vse8.v
 *          v0..v31 to the V-scratch region.
 *   QEMU2: vle8.v v0..v31 from V-scratch (round-trip the regfile back into
 *          QEMU's V state), then vse32.v v8..v15 + verify; v8/v10..v15
 *          unchanged from seed, v9 == seed + 100.
 *
 * V-state transport
 *   The vsim hart's V regfile is not exposed via Debug Module Abstract
 *   Commands (the spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer / DPI). As a
 *   stop-gap we use a fixed scratch region inside the shared mmap (V_SCRATCH
 *   below, matching hybrid::kVScratchGpa). vsim's StateDrain reads those
 *   bytes into hybrid_state_v1::vreg[] so qemu_handback can ship them to
 *   QEMU 2 via existing gdbstub P packets. ResumeDriver does the inverse on
 *   the way in, but this fixture's QEMU1 phase doesn't use V so the
 *   inbound V state is all zeros -- vsim re-seeds v0..v31 itself by
 *   loading from the SEED region.
 *
 * Memory layout:
 *   SEED / PHASE_C_OUT       - .bss, preserved by the "no redundant ELF
 *                              reload" rule (vsim skips load_elf and
 *                              QEMU 2 drops -kernel under shared mmap).
 *   V_SCRATCH at 0x80FF0000  - matches hybrid::kVScratchGpa (32 V regs *
 *                              64 bytes = 2048 B). Fixed by the ABI
 *                              between vsim's V transport and this
 *                              fixture's vsim-phase asm; cannot move.
 *   stack at 0x80700000      - rt_c.ld places _stack here
 *
 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512 (vlenb=64). QEMU launched with
 * -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512; vsim built from
 * data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg with NDS_VLEN 512.
 */
#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"

#include <stdint.h>
#include <stdio.h>

#define VL_E32 16  /* VLEN/32 = 512/32 = 16 int32 elements per V reg at LMUL=1 */
#define VLENB  64  /* bytes per V reg */
#define NREGS  8   /* test v8..v15 */

static int32_t SEED[VL_E32];
static int32_t PHASE_C_OUT[NREGS * VL_E32];

#define V_SCRATCH 0x80FF0000UL

static void seed_pattern(int32_t* dst, int n)
{
  for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
    dst[i] = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5);
}

int main(void)
{
  /* Phase 1 (QEMU1): seed memory and exercise V instructions immediately
   * before the drain marker. Historically this triggered a plugin bug
   * (gdbstub PC readback lagged when V was in flight) that misreported the
   * drain PC. Now the plugin captures vaddr+size at translate time
   * (HandoffSite), so V activity here is safe. v0 is overwritten in Phase 2
   * by the vle32.v on v0..v31, so the value loaded here is irrelevant to
   * the test outcome -- the goal is purely to retire V insns ahead of the
   * trigger. */
  seed_pattern(SEED, VL_E32);

  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v0, (t0)\n"
    "vadd.vi v0, v0, 7\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory", "t0", "a4");

  enter_vsim();

  /* Phase 2 (vsim): load v8..v15 from SEED, mutate v9, then save the full
   * v0..v31 regfile to V_SCRATCH so vsim's drain can read them out for
   * the QEMU 2 hop. mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver (Tier-A). */
  asm volatile(
    /* Reload v8..v15 from SEED via 32-bit unit-stride loads. */
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "mv     t0, %0\n"
    "vle32.v v8,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v9,  (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v10, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v11, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v12, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v13, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v14, (t0)\n"
    "vle32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    /* Mutate v9 by +100 (vadd.vi takes 5-bit signed imm; +10 ten times). */
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    "vadd.vi v9, v9, 10\n"
    /* Save v0..v31 to V_SCRATCH for vsim's drain (e8 stride to write
     * exactly vlenb bytes per reg, regardless of element semantics). */
    "li     a4, 64\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e8, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "li     t0, 0x80FF0000\n"
    "vse8.v v0,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v1,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v2,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v3,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v4,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v5,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v6,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v7,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v15, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v16, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v17, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v18, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v19, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v20, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v21, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v22, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v23, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v24, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v25, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v26, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v27, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v28, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v29, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v30, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse8.v v31, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(SEED)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  exit_vsim();

  /* Phase 3 (QEMU2): the gdbstub P packets (sent by qemu_handback) have
   * already populated v0..v31 in QEMU's V state from state.vreg[]. Run
   * vse32.v v8..v15 to PHASE_C_OUT and verify the bytes match the seed
   * pattern (with v9 +100).
   *
   * mstatus.VS is restored by ResumeDriver so no csrs needed. QEMU 2
   * must be spawned with -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 (see
   * qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu) - the default rv64 CPU has VS as
   * read-only zero and vsetvli would trap. */
  asm volatile(
    "li     a4, 16\n"
    "vsetvli zero, a4, e32, m1, ta, ma\n"
    "addi t0, %0, 0\n"
    "vse32.v v8,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v9,  (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v10, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v11, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v12, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v13, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v14, (t0)\n addi t0, t0, 64\n"
    "vse32.v v15, (t0)\n"
    :: "r"(PHASE_C_OUT)
    : "memory","t0","a4");

  for (int reg = 0; reg < NREGS; reg++) {
    int32_t* row = PHASE_C_OUT + reg * VL_E32;
    int32_t bias = (reg == 1) ? 100 : 0;  /* v9 is reg index 1 in v8..v15 */
    for (int i = 0; i < VL_E32; i++) {
      int32_t expected = (int32_t) ((i * 17) ^ 0xA5A5) + bias;
      if (row[i] != expected) {
        printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL\n");
        fflush(stdout);
        return 1;
      }
    }
  }
  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
  fflush(stdout);
  return 0;
}

content.content:
1	// V-register transport via shared scratch RAM. The vsim Debug Module
2	// abstract-command space does not allocate slots for v0..v31 (RISC-V Debug
3	// Spec leaves V to optional Program Buffer or implementation-specific
4	// extensions), and the AX45MPV CPU wrapper does not export a DPI probe for
5	// the V regfile. As a stop-gap that keeps Phase 5 (DPI / PB) deferred while
6	// still letting C-level RVV fixtures round-trip:
7	//
8	//   - On resume from QEMU 1, vsim copies state.vreg[] into a fixed scratch
9	//     region of guest RAM (kVScratchGpa). The fixture's vsim-phase prologue
10	//     issues `vle8.v vN, (t0)` against that region to repopulate v0..v31.
11	//   - Before the vsim->QEMU2 drain trigger, the fixture issues `vse8.v vN,
12	//     (t0)` to write the (possibly mutated) regfile back to the same region.
13	//     vsim's StateDrain side reads the scratch bytes into state.vreg[] so
14	//     qemu_handback can ship them to QEMU 2 via the existing gdbstub P
15	//     packets.
16	//
17	// The contract is fixture-aware: any program that wants V state preserved
18	// across the vsim boundary must perform the load/store at the boundaries.
19	// rt_c_v_regs.c demonstrates the pattern. Phase 5 will replace this with a
20	// transparent mechanism (PB or DPI) so benchmark binaries don't need to be
21	// modified.
22	//
23	// Pure logic: no SystemC, no DMI bus. Easy to unit-test.
24	
25	#pragma once
26	
27	#include "hybrid/state_abi.h"
28	
29	#include <cstddef>
30	#include <cstdint>
31	#include <cstring>
32	#include <span>
33	#include <stdexcept>
34	
35	namespace hybrid
36	{
37	
38	// Top of QEMU virt's default 128 MiB RAM (RAM_BASE 0x80000000 + 0x0FF0000
39	// = 0x80FF0000). Below the C-runtime stack at 0x80700000 only when the
40	// runtime grows beyond default; rt_c.ld places _stack at 0x80700000 so
41	// 0x80FF0000 sits well above it. Document the reservation in fixture
42	// comments so future memory-layout changes don't collide.
43	inline constexpr uint64_t kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000ull;
44	
45	// Total scratch bytes needed: 32 V regs × HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Sized to
46	// the maximum supported VLEN; the actual per-reg byte count is `vlenb`.
47	inline constexpr std::size_t kVScratchBytes =
48	  static_cast<std::size_t>(32) * HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX;
49	
50	namespace detail
51	{
52	
53	// Translate scratch GPA into a byte offset inside a RAM span whose first
54	// byte sits at `ram_base`. Throws if the scratch region falls outside the
55	// supplied span (i.e. the caller passed a too-small RAM mapping).
56	inline auto scratch_offset(std::size_t ram_size, uint64_t ram_base)
57	  -> std::size_t
58	{
59	  if (kVScratchGpa < ram_base)
60	    throw std::runtime_error(
61	      "v_regfile_xfer: scratch GPA below RAM base");
62	  const auto off = static_cast<std::size_t>(kVScratchGpa - ram_base);
63	  if (off + kVScratchBytes > ram_size)
64	    throw std::runtime_error(
65	      "v_regfile_xfer: scratch region exceeds RAM span");
66	  return off;
67	}
68	
69	}  // namespace detail
70	
71	// Copy state.vreg[] into ram[scratch_offset .. scratch_offset+kVScratchBytes].
72	// Called just before vsim resumes the hart so the fixture can vle8.v from
73	// scratch and pick up QEMU 1's V values.
74	inline void stage_v_to_scratch(const hybrid_state_v1& state,
75	                               std::span<uint8_t> ram,
76	                               uint64_t ram_base)
77	{
78	  const auto off = detail::scratch_offset(ram.size(), ram_base);
79	  std::memcpy(ram.data() + off, state.vreg, kVScratchBytes);
80	}
81	
82	// Copy ram[scratch_offset .. +kVScratchBytes] into state.vreg[]. Called
83	// inside the vsim drain path after the fixture's prologue vse8.v has
84	// settled the (possibly mutated) regfile bytes into RAM.
85	inline void read_v_from_scratch(hybrid_state_v1& state,
86	                                std::span<const uint8_t> ram,
87	                                uint64_t ram_base)
88	{
89	  const auto off = detail::scratch_offset(ram.size(), ram_base);
90	  std::memcpy(state.vreg, ram.data() + off, kVScratchBytes);
91	}
92	
93	}  // namespace hybrid
94

content.text:
State transfer between QEMU 1 and vsim in `rt_c_v_matmul.c` splits into two pipelines: memory state rides the shared mmap; V state rides the drain/resume binary plus a scratch region (and rt_c_v_matmul deliberately doesn't load V regs back).

**Memory state (A, B, C)**

- A/B/C are `static int32_t A[N*N]` in `.bss`, so the toolchain just gives them addresses in `0x80000000+` RAM.
- Tier-2 harness (`rt_shared_mem.sh`) allocates a file in `/dev/shm` via `mktemp` + `truncate`. Both QEMUs and vsim are launched against the same file:
  - QEMU1/QEMU2: `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`
  - vsim: `--shared-mem-path` mmaps the file as its `main_memory`.
- Phase 1 writes by `init_matrices` go straight into that shared file via QEMU's RAM-backed store path.
- Phase 2 vsim's main memory IS that mmap. The "no redundant ELF reload" guard:
  - `src/main.cpp:406-414` sets `skip_memory_load=true` when `--resume-from-qemu` and `--shared-mem-path` are both set.
  - `src/simulator.hpp:267-289` still parses the ELF for the entrypoint (reset vector) but skips `read_elf_into_memory`, so phase-1 bytes aren't clobbered.
- QEMU2: `qemu_handback.hpp:151-160` drops `-kernel <elf>` when `shared_mem_path` is non-empty, same reason.

Result: A,B,C are one physical buffer all three phases observe; no copy.

**V register state (QEMU 1 -> vsim)**

The Debug Module abstract-command space doesn't expose `v0..v31`, so the transport is split:

1. **Plugin drain (QEMU 1)**: `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` snapshots `vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr` into `hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`), gated by `HYBRID_FLAG_V`.
2. **Stage V regfile to RAM (vsim entry)**: `simulator.hpp:208` calls `hybrid::stage_v_to_scratch` which `memcpy`s `state.vreg[]` into the shared mmap at `kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000` (see `src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp:74-80`).
3. **CSR restore via DMI (vsim entry)**: `ResumeDriver`:
   - Tier-A Abstract Commands: `mstatus.VS`, `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`.
   - Tier-B Program Buffer: stages `vl` in x5 and `vtype` in x6, then runs `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (only way to restore both atomically; direct CSR write to `vtype` sets `vill`).
   - Order matters: VS -> vsetvl -> vstart (vsetvl resets vstart) -> other V CSRs -> GPRs.
4. **Load V regfile from scratch (fixture)**: a `vle8.v vN, (t0)` prologue would pull the staged bytes into `v0..v31`. **rt_c_v_matmul does not do this** -- `vmatmul_kernel` reinitializes V state itself (`vsetvl_e32m1` then `vmv_v_x_i32m1(0,...)` per row), so QEMU1's V regfile contents are dropped on the floor. Only the CSR side (especially `mstatus.VS`) needs to survive.

If you want the V regfile itself round-tripped, see `rt_c_v_regs.c:84-128` for the explicit `vle32.v`/`vse8.v` against `V_SCRATCH` -- that's the fixture-aware contract documented at the top of `v_regfile_xfer.hpp`.
